response:
I checked the first two rounds in detail.

Round 0 (lines L0069–L0075)  
• h = 0x5BE0CD19 (line L0069)  
• S1 = 0x3587272B (line L0070)  
  h + S1  = 0x5BE0CD19 + 0x3587272B = 0x9167F444, exactly the value shown for step1 (line L0072).  
Everything that follows in the round is therefore consistent.

Round 1 (lines L0083–L0089)  
• h = 0x1F83D9AB  (line L0083)  
• S1 = 0x21D43462  (line L0084)  

Adding them should give  

 0x1F83D9AB + 0x21D43462 = 0x41480E0D  

but the trace lists step1 (line L0086) as  

 0x41580E0D  

The difference is exactly 0x01000000 (a single-bit flip at bit-24).  
All later values in the round (step2, step3, temp1, …) are arithmetically consistent with this wrong step1, and the register shift at line L0095 uses those tampered results, so the trace stays self-consistent afterwards.

Therefore the single-bit fault occurs in round 1, at step1 (line L0086).

VERDICT: TAMPERED  
ROUND: 1  
REASONING: step1 in round 1 (line L0086) should equal h + S1 from lines L0083–L0084.  
Expected sum: 0x41480E0D; trace shows 0x41580E0D – one bit different – proving a single-bit tamper starting in round 1.
